title: tryonia sellowiana
intro: Tryonia sellowiana, also known as the "Cocha" or "Cocha de muñeco", is a type of shrub native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of Tryonia sellowiana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used as an ingredient in traditional remedies.
